rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as constants/variables.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning declared types with actual expression types (int, real, bool).
Avoid using package-qualified names in AGREE code that are not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced features and variables are declared or visible in the same annex scope.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE constant and equation declarations.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation contracts in implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Equation declarations in AGREE must have consistent left and right-hand side types.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current AGREE annex scope.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions (e.g., int and real) without explicit conversion.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Component port assumptions and guarantees in type scopes
